lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska is here. I've shown her into the meeting room.

lines: Good morning. I am Halina Sikorska. I have been told you can help.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good morning, Mrs. Sikorska. Dr. A. Cula. Please, sit. Thank you for coming in.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: This is Mr. Crab and Mr. Whimsy. They have read the file. You know Mr. Murrow.

lines: Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska.

lines.speaker: whimsy
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska — a pleasure. Forgive the assembly. The file deserves it.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The notice and the docket are in the file. Cula will lead.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. Before we go through the documents, how are you holding up? The last three weeks.

lines: Thank you for asking. The matter has occupied more of my attention than I would have chosen. I have done what was needed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the apartment itself. How long.

lines: My father had the lease before me. He worked on the railways. He was given the apartment when he was assigned to Warsaw, in nineteen sixty-two. I inherited the lease in nineteen eighty-seven, after his death. I have lived there since. My husband moved in with me in nineteen seventy-four. He died in two thousand and nine. My son is in Wrocław. He has his own life. Iwaszkiewicz wrote that buildings hold us longer than we hold them. He may have been generous to the buildings.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. And your work.

lines: I taught Polish for thirty-eight years. Mostly in the same lyceum. The students change. The texts do not.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The current tenancy paperwork. May I see it.

lines: Of course.
The current paperwork is here. The address as it has stood since the renumbering.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the notice.

lines: Here.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the court docket. Mr. Murrow obtained it this morning.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The hearing has already been held. Ex parte. You were not present.

lines: I was not informed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: No. You were not.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: There is a person in your building. Number seven. The current resident. You mentioned him on the phone.

lines: Yes. He brought the notice down to me. He had opened the envelope, read the first line. He apologised. He left it on the kitchen table and went back upstairs. He is a kind young man. He is not at fault for opening it.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And anyone else in the building who has known you at that address. For some time.

lines: Mrs. Wójcik. Second floor. She has lived there since nineteen ninety-one. She buys her bread at the same shop as I do. She would know me from the corridor; she would know my mother's name, if it were asked of her, because I told her once. She would sign a statement, if a statement were needed. I asked her, two weeks ago. She offered.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: The neighbour's statement. Continuous resident, second floor, since nineteen ninety-one. We can take that.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. We will take it. Has she been at home this week.

lines: She has. She will be at home on Friday afternoon. She is patient. She has been a widow longer than I have been one.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And your own movements. This week. I will be brief.

lines: I have been at the apartment most days. Tuesday morning at the doctor. Thursday afternoon at the library. I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. I have what I need from this part of the conversation.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The current tenancy paperwork. The defective notice. The court docket. Mr. Murrow has the file.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: We will file the motion to set aside on the ground of invalid service. The hearing will be rescheduled. You will not need to be present at the motion itself; you may need to be present at the rescheduled hearing, depending on what the landlord does next.

lines: Thank you. I will be available either way.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The fee for the motion and the rescheduled hearing is five thousand. Fixed. It includes the filing, the appearance, and any drafting necessary if the landlord answers.

lines: Five thousand. I have the money. I will transfer it before Friday.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The retention is ready for signature. I will bring it through.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Mr. Murrow. The Hennessy retainer phone has gone overboard. Third time this morning. Where is Asia.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: And the printer is throwing red lights. Small craft warning, Mr. Murrow —

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Mr. Pig. A moment. The meeting room.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The printer. Now.

lines: I gather Mr. Pig is the senior partner.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: He is.

lines: I gather the firm is busy.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: My apologies, Mrs. Sikorska. The Hennessy retainer phone is a long-running matter.

lines: Of course.
You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Two signatures. Here and here. I will witness.

lines: Thank you.
Until Friday.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Until Friday. Thank you, Mrs. Sikorska.

lines: Thank you, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy. Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska is here. I've shown her into the meeting room.

lines: Good morning. I am Halina Sikorska. I have been told you can help.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good morning, Mrs. Sikorska. Dr. A. Cula. Please, sit. Thank you for coming in.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: This is Mr. Crab and Mr. Whimsy. They have read the file. You know Mr. Murrow.

lines: Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska.

lines.speaker: whimsy
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska — a pleasure. Forgive the assembly. The file deserves it.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The notice and the docket are in the file. Cula will lead.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. I will go through this in order. The notice — when was it dated.

lines: The eighth of April.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: When did you receive it.

lines: The twenty-eighth. Twenty days later.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: How.

lines: The current resident of number seven brought it down. The envelope had been opened. He had read the first line before realising it was not for him. He apologised at the door.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The number on the envelope.

lines: Number seven. The address as it stood after the building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. My apartment is now number twelve. It was number seven before the renumbering.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the landlord. He sent the notice to number seven knowing the renumbering had occurred.

lines: He knew. He ought to have known. I cannot prove what he knew. I can prove what he was told.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: What was he told.

lines: Six months ago he sent another letter. A rent statement. It came back to him. The post returned it to the sender. I know this because I telephoned his office about a separate matter a week later, and the woman who answered the telephone said the letter had been returned and that they would resend to the correct address. I do not know whether they did. I have no copy. I do not have the slip. It would be in his office, if he kept it.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: The return-to-sender slip. If it is in his file, it is producible by request. The slip alone is enough on knowledge.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Producible by request. We will request it.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Did the office that called you give a date for the previous letter.

lines: October. Late October. I do not have it written down. I would estimate the twentieth or the twenty-fifth.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. The current notice. You read it on the twenty-eighth of April.

lines: I read it that evening. I telephoned the firm the following morning. Mr. Murrow returned my call that afternoon. I came in the following Tuesday — last Tuesday — for the brief consultation. Today is the formal meeting.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the current tenancy paperwork.

lines: Renewed in two thousand and nineteen. The address as it stands since the renumbering.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the court docket.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The docket lists a hearing. It has already been held. Ex parte. You were not present.

lines: I was not informed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: No. You were not.

lines: I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. The motion will be filed before any new hearing date is assigned. You will not need to be present at the motion itself.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: whimsy
lines.text: A procedural defect dressed in a residential coat. Article 135-bis carries the doctrine; Article 132 carries the service. The combination is the case. Verily.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. I have the timeline.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The current tenancy paperwork. The defective notice. The court docket. Mr. Murrow has the file.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: We will file the motion to set aside on the ground of invalid service. The hearing will be rescheduled. You will not need to be present at the motion itself; you may need to be present at the rescheduled hearing, depending on what the landlord does next.

lines: Thank you. I will be available either way.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The fee for the motion and the rescheduled hearing is five thousand. Fixed. It includes the filing, the appearance, and any drafting necessary if the landlord answers.

lines: Five thousand. I have the money. I will transfer it before Friday.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The retention is ready for signature. I will bring it through.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Mr. Murrow. The Hennessy retainer phone has gone overboard. Third time this morning. Where is Asia.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: And the printer is throwing red lights. Small craft warning, Mr. Murrow —

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Mr. Pig. A moment. The meeting room.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The printer. Now.

lines: I gather Mr. Pig is the senior partner.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: He is.

lines: I gather the firm is busy.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: My apologies, Mrs. Sikorska. The Hennessy retainer phone is a long-running matter.

lines: Of course.
You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Two signatures. Here and here. I will witness.

lines: Thank you.
Until Friday.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Until Friday. Thank you, Mrs. Sikorska.

lines: Thank you, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy. Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska is here. I've shown her into the meeting room.

lines: Good morning. I am Halina Sikorska. I have been told you can help.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good morning, Mrs. Sikorska. Dr. A. Cula. Please, sit. Thank you for coming in.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: This is Mr. Crab and Mr. Whimsy. They have read the file. You know Mr. Murrow.

lines: Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska.

lines.speaker: whimsy
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska — a pleasure. Forgive the assembly. The file deserves it.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The notice and the docket are in the file. Cula will lead.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. The lease history. How far back does your tenancy run.

lines: Nineteen sixty-two. My father held the original lease. I inherited in nineteen eighty-seven.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Do you have the original.

lines: I have it here.
This is the original lease. My father's name. Nineteen sixty-two. The signature is the building's administrator at the time. The seal is the housing cooperative's.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Two reorganisations of the cooperative since then. Nineteen seventy-four and two thousand and four. The chain holds across both.

lines: Yes.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the inheritance paperwork.

lines: Nineteen eighty-seven. After my father's death. The document is here.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. The chain is clean.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: It is.

lines: The current rental agreement renewal is from two thousand and nineteen. It has my address as it stands since the renumbering. Number twelve. The renumbering was in two thousand and fifteen.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the notice.

lines: Number seven. The address as it stood before the renumbering. The landlord sent it to an address that has not been mine for eleven years.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. And the court docket.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The hearing has already been held. Ex parte. You were not present.

lines: I was not informed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: No. You were not.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: One last thing. The cooperative office — your records. Are they accessible if we need to verify a chain detail before Thursday.

lines: The cooperative office is open weekday mornings. I can be there tomorrow if needed. I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me. The cooperative is two stops on the tram.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. We will not trouble them unless the landlord answers on the underlying tenancy. He will not. The procedural defect will close the matter at the motion.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: The chain is clean through both reorganisations. The procedural defect ends the landlord's case before he reaches the merits.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: It does.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. The chain is what I needed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The current tenancy paperwork. The defective notice. The court docket. Mr. Murrow has the file.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: We will file the motion to set aside on the ground of invalid service. The hearing will be rescheduled. You will not need to be present at the motion itself; you may need to be present at the rescheduled hearing, depending on what the landlord does next.

lines: Thank you. I will be available either way.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The fee for the motion and the rescheduled hearing is five thousand. Fixed. It includes the filing, the appearance, and any drafting necessary if the landlord answers.

lines: Five thousand. I have the money. I will transfer it before Friday.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The retention is ready for signature. I will bring it through.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Mr. Murrow. The Hennessy retainer phone has gone overboard. Third time this morning. Where is Asia.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: And the printer is throwing red lights. Small craft warning, Mr. Murrow —

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Mr. Pig. A moment. The meeting room.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The printer. Now.

lines: I gather Mr. Pig is the senior partner.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: He is.

lines: I gather the firm is busy.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: My apologies, Mrs. Sikorska. The Hennessy retainer phone is a long-running matter.

lines: Of course.
You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Two signatures. Here and here. I will witness.

lines: Thank you.
Until Friday.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Until Friday. Thank you, Mrs. Sikorska.

lines: Thank you, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy. Mr. Murrow.
